title: Self Deportation

jokes.text: Ron Howard wants to make a heartfelt father-son drama that has a good clean message for his illegitimate daughter to star in.
jokes.setup: Producer explains Ron Howard wants to make a wholesome family film
jokes.punchline: The contradictory detail that it's for his illegitimate daughter
jokes.explanation: Sharp ironic contradiction - wholesome message undermined by illegitimate daughter detail. Classic AD layered writing.

jokes.text: he's already started paying off -the Hollywood Foreign Press. -No, they're great. - She's funny.
jokes.setup: Producer mentions bribing the Hollywood Foreign Press
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to walk back the bribery comment with weak praise
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about award show corruption with realistic awkward recovery attempt.

jokes.text: I want to remake Spider-Man 2, the first one.
jokes.setup: Producer explains his dream superhero movie
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical description of wanting to remake Spider-Man 2, calling it 'the first one'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economical absurdity - the contradiction is delivered matter-of-factly, making it funnier.

jokes.text: My brother Buster has something better than a hook. A robot hand.
jokes.setup: Movie needs a hook
jokes.punchline: Michael suggests Buster's robot hand is literally better than a hook
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'hook' as movie concept vs. Buster's literal missing hand. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: You're out of the movie. You're out of the movie. He hadn't destroyed. You're out of the movie. You're out of the movie! Know what? You're out of the movie. Fine.
jokes.setup: Producer gets increasingly agitated with Michael
jokes.punchline: Repetitive escalation of 'You're out of the movie' followed by Michael's calm 'Fine'
jokes.explanation: Repetition creates escalation but lacks originality. Michael's understated response provides good contrast.

jokes.text: I get paid? They're Imagine dollars. They're redeemable in the gift shop. That's Ron Howard. Brian's on the 50.
jokes.setup: Michael asks about payment for the movie
jokes.punchline: Fake currency with Ron Howard and Brian Grazer on the bills
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - company scrip with real producer faces. Very AD-style layered absurdity.

jokes.text: It's a severance thing they're doing because of Ron's need to be loved and Brian's conflict avoidance issues.
jokes.setup: Explaining the fake money system
jokes.punchline: Psychoanalyzing the real producers' personalities as business reasoning
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about the show's actual producers presented as casual psychological insight.

jokes.text: Oh, we only sell these as a set. They were getting very competitive. Were they? Brian was.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about buying items separately
jokes.punchline: The casual gossip about Brian Grazer being competitive
jokes.explanation: The mundane delivery of celebrity gossip works well

jokes.text: Winner of 1982's Least Seen on Campus.
jokes.setup: Buster mentions being a Milford man
jokes.punchline: Proudly citing an award for being invisible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster line - absurd pride in being unnoticeable, perfectly integrated with his character.

jokes.text: I wish I could've seen their faces at the ceremony.
jokes.setup: Buster won award for being least seen
jokes.punchline: The irony that he wishes he could have seen the ceremony for an invisibility award
jokes.explanation: Excellent follow-up joke with perfect ironic logic. The contradiction is both obvious and brilliant.

jokes.text: This is one of only two things the Army warns us about when they issue these. Boy, Kitty does not know what she's sitting on.
jokes.setup: Buster warns about the robot hand being dangerous
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates the danger while making innuendo about Kitty
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with Buster's characteristic obliviousness to innuendo while creating it.

jokes.text: Maybe that's a role I can play in your life. Don't say... Mom. Mmm.
jokes.setup: Michael offers to be a parental figure to Buster
jokes.punchline: Buster chooses 'Mom' instead of 'Dad'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster character moment - his psychological issues create the uncomfortable comedy.

jokes.text: I ain't got no truck with no blue.
jokes.setup: Buster panics about talking to cops
jokes.punchline: Uses street slang completely out of character for him
jokes.explanation: Hilarious character contradiction - sheltered Buster trying to sound street smart. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Well, I lived with Mother. Did you think we were dating?
jokes.setup: Michael mentions Buster lived with Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Buster's confusion about which Lucille, implying he might date his mother
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but perfectly in-character moment highlighting Buster's disturbing mother fixation.

jokes.text: Question's been asked.
jokes.setup: Buster asks if people think he dates his mother
jokes.punchline: Michael's deadpan confirmation that people have wondered
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and deadpan delivery. Michael's uncomfortable honesty creates the comedy.

jokes.text: No! No! A thousand times, no! Is that what they'll ask? 'Cause it's not like I'm gonna remember details about where I saw her and what we fought about.
jokes.setup: Buster denies knowing where Lucille 2 is
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he does remember details while claiming he doesn't
jokes.explanation: Classic self-incrimination structure - Buster's panic makes him reveal what he's trying to hide.

jokes.text: You kept me from Mother? You sent her to prison? Should I not say that?
jokes.setup: Buster continues revealing what he fought about with Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's saying incriminating things and asks if he should stop
jokes.explanation: Continues the self-incrimination pattern. Buster's awareness of his mistake makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Should I not say that?
jokes.setup: Buster just revealed incriminating information
jokes.punchline: He belatedly realizes he shouldn't have said it
jokes.explanation: Good timing on the reaction. Shows Buster's delayed realization of consequences.

jokes.text: Just Mama's worst nightmare. Buster's missing. Oh, and his bath has gone cold.
jokes.setup: Michael describes the plan to worry his mother
jokes.punchline: The mundane detail about cold bathwater as equally concerning
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight - the cold bath detail shows how coddled Buster is.

jokes.text: Oh, those things are so easy to beat.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests Buster get a psych evaluation
jokes.punchline: Buster casually reveals he's good at fooling psychological tests
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation delivered casually. Implies Buster has experience with psychological evaluation.

jokes.text: oh, let's say, boarding school romance.
jokes.setup: Narrator describes G.O.B.'s relationship with Tony Wonder
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of their sexual relationship
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism delivered in the narrator's characteristic hesitant style.

jokes.text: cincos and mayos. We need to find out whatever the word for ten is. Find that. It's ten. Every culture uses ten.
jokes.setup: G.O.B. and George Sr. rating women using Spanish numbers
jokes.punchline: G.O.B.'s ignorance that 'ten' is universal
jokes.explanation: Good character-based stupidity. G.O.B.'s cultural ignorance creates the absurdity.

jokes.text: It blew off at the CVS parking lot.
jokes.setup: Discussing George Sr.'s recent changes
jokes.punchline: Random mention of something (presumably his toupee) blowing away
jokes.explanation: Mundane location makes the image funnier. Implies George Sr.'s vanity about his appearance.

jokes.text: That's my penis. You don't have to tell me.
jokes.setup: Therapist points to a spot on George Sr.'s body
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s matter-of-fact response about his anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable humor. George Sr.'s blunt response to an awkward situation.

jokes.text: I can get hard, so let's focus on you.
jokes.setup: George Sr. discussing his sexual problems
jokes.punchline: Dismissive attitude toward therapy while oversharing
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable oversharing presented as helpfulness. Classic George Sr. selfishness.

jokes.text: They're primas. Primos, huh? I've gotta be honest, I've seen better. No, it means cousins.
jokes.setup: Bartender explains women are cousins (primas)
jokes.punchline: G.O.B. and George Sr. think it's a rating system
jokes.explanation: Good misunderstanding joke based on character ignorance and narcissism.

jokes.text: APB. 'Aw, poor Buster.'
jokes.setup: Michael mentions putting out an APB
jokes.punchline: Buster interprets the acronym as sympathetic words
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures Buster's need for sympathy and attention.

jokes.text: You always come back to save the family. We joke about that all the time.
jokes.setup: Michael says he might not come back
jokes.punchline: Buster reveals the family mocks Michael's hero complex
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the show's basic premise delivered through character obliviousness.

jokes.text: 'Hi, I'm Michael,' and I save everyone so I feel special, even though you don't deserve it.
jokes.setup: Buster imitating the family's jokes about Michael
jokes.punchline: Accurate but cruel impression of Michael's martyr complex
jokes.explanation: Devastating character analysis delivered innocently by Buster. Meta-commentary on the show's structure.

jokes.text: Covered a lot of ground there.
jokes.setup: Buster's long, disturbing family impression
jokes.punchline: Michael's understated response to the emotional chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction. Michael's understated response to family trauma is both funny and sad.

jokes.text: I saw Lucille 2's lifeless body sprawled across the stair car?
jokes.setup: Michael tells Buster to tell the truth to police
jokes.punchline: Buster casually mentions seeing Lucille 2's dead body
jokes.explanation: Shocking dark revelation delivered casually. Major plot point revealed as throwaway example.

jokes.text: Perfect. If that's what happened, tell them.
jokes.setup: Buster mentions seeing Lucille 2's dead body
jokes.punchline: Michael's calm acceptance of potential murder confession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan reaction to shocking information. Michael's obliviousness creates dark comedy.

jokes.text: This is literally one of only two things the Army warns about when they issue these.
jokes.setup: Buster struggling with his robot hand
jokes.punchline: Second mention of mysterious Army warnings
jokes.explanation: Running gag setup. The specificity makes it funnier - what's the other thing?

jokes.text: This is why you always... leave a note. Always leave a note. Where's that from?
jokes.setup: Michael having communication problems
jokes.punchline: References famous series catchphrase but can't remember where it's from
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about the show's own catchphrase. Michael's confusion adds another layer.

jokes.text: Welcome to the shitty parents club. No, no, we don't have kids.
jokes.setup: Maeby welcomes George Michael to the 'shitty parents club'
jokes.punchline: George Michael's confusion about whether it's for bad parents or people with bad parents
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay misunderstanding. The ambiguity of the phrase creates the comedy.

jokes.text: Mexico sounds far from Perfecto. So that's perfect.
jokes.setup: George Michael mentions going to Mexico
jokes.punchline: Maeby's play on 'perfecto' meaning it's perfect because it's imperfect
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that fits Maeby's character and motivation to escape.

jokes.text: Just the cousins. Primos! This crew. Why do you keep yelling that? Stop it.
jokes.setup: George Michael and Maeby decide to go together
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier 'primos' joke, George Michael's confusion
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that connects storylines. Not particularly strong on its own.

jokes.text: Oh, no. They never arrest the drivers. They do come down pretty hard on the cars, but it's cheaper to just retrain the people instead.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if he's under arrest for taking the Google car
jokes.punchline: Dystopian corporate logic treating people as more replaceable than cars
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dystopian satire delivered matter-of-factly. Commentary on corporate dehumanization.

jokes.text: It sounds super futuristic and dystopian, but it's really not.
jokes.setup: Describing the corporate policy of punishing cars instead of people
jokes.punchline: Claiming it's not dystopian when it obviously is
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contradiction. The denial makes the dystopian nature more obvious.

jokes.text: Primos stick together. Oh. 'Cousin.' It means 'cousins.' That makes sense.
jokes.setup: George Michael uses 'primos' incorrectly
jokes.punchline: Finally learns what it actually means
jokes.explanation: Payoff to running gag. Adds irony since they actually are cousins.

jokes.text: ♪ Mr. Efe ♪ Play the guitar, old man! Strum! Strum!
jokes.setup: Threatening horsemen confronting the travelers
jokes.punchline: Demanding musical performance instead of violence
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation of violence with absurd musical demand. Random but effective.

jokes.text: They just looked like old white men to me.
jokes.setup: George Michael describes the 'scary, handsome Mexicans'
jokes.punchline: Maeby's matter-of-fact deflation of his romanticizing
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of George Michael's romanticism. Maeby's practical perspective creates humor.

jokes.text: Who were those scary, handsome Mexicans on those magical horses? They just looked like old white men to me.
jokes.setup: George Michael's romantic description of the horsemen
jokes.punchline: Maeby's deflating observation about their actual appearance
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between romantic perception and mundane reality

jokes.text: No, that one was empty. I found it in 'Just Odds and Ends.' Great, you got past my double bluff.
jokes.setup: Maeby found George Michael's secrets
jokes.punchline: George Michael's pathetic filing system and 'double bluff'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Michael's overthinking and Maeby's directness.

jokes.text: Great, you got past my double bluff.
jokes.setup: His folder system being discovered
jokes.punchline: Treating his poor file organization as a sophisticated strategy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Michael's self-justification

jokes.text: Maybe it's 'go-op'? To me, that would make less sense. Maybe that's what 'go-op' is. Not everything always making sense.
jokes.setup: Discussion about what 'goop' means
jokes.punchline: Circular nonsensical conversation about meaningless terms
jokes.explanation: Captures the absurdity of millennial lifestyle trends but runs a bit long.

jokes.text: Bush. Trash bag and bush. Thought it was a woman. Like, hunched over, right?
jokes.setup: G.O.B. and George Sr. checking out what they think is a woman
jokes.punchline: It's just a trash bag and some vegetation
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing their desperation and poor eyesight. Somewhat predictable but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: That was a ten-gallon Hefty sack? Oh, yeah. Yeah, a ten. Definitely a ten.
jokes.setup: Realizing they were attracted to a trash bag
jokes.punchline: Still rating the trash bag as a 'ten'
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing their commitment to the rating system even for inanimate objects.

jokes.text: I still think you're gonna have a problem with this final 'raped murderer.'
jokes.setup: Tobias reviewing Lucille's complaints about people
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of someone being a 'raped murderer'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered in Tobias's inappropriately casual therapeutic manner.

jokes.text: My daughter is divorcing you. This is it. I'm confused by the word 'it.' I'm confused by the word 'anymore.'
jokes.setup: Lucille trying to fire Tobias as her therapist
jokes.punchline: Tobias's literal interpretation of simple words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias character moment - his therapeutic training makes him annoyingly literal.

jokes.text: Well, this is America. They don't want us to burn signs, they shouldn't write 'em in Spanish.
jokes.setup: Oscar burning signs on the border
jokes.punchline: Racist logic for destroying Spanish-language signs
jokes.explanation: Dark satirical take on xenophobia delivered through Oscar's clueless character.

jokes.text: I always say, 'Make people cry, make people cry.'
jokes.setup: Tobias says 'hurt people hurt people'
jokes.punchline: Lucille's twisted version focusing on making others cry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille line - takes therapeutic wisdom and makes it cruelly self-serving. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Puta Night is Thursday. That's so nice. No, I'm not. That was based on her belief that 'puta' meant 'young.'
jokes.setup: Man tells Lindsay about 'Puta Night'
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's innocent misunderstanding of what 'puta' means
jokes.explanation: Good character-based misunderstanding. Lindsay's vanity creates the innocent confusion.

jokes.text: Oh. I thought you were just trying to get me to give you a higher Uber rating.
jokes.setup: Learning the volunteers are actually helping refugees
jokes.punchline: Maeby thought the humanitarian work was for ride ratings
jokes.explanation: Great modern comedy - reduces humanitarian crisis to gig economy concerns. Very Maeby.

jokes.text: It's great because it's subtitled in Spanish, but we don't have to read. It's in English.
jokes.setup: Americans watching Minions movie in Mexico
jokes.punchline: Proud of not having to read subtitles for a wordless movie
jokes.explanation: Good satire of American cultural ignorance abroad. The Minions detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Which was my idea! And I will have Mexico pay for that wall. That is a clever twist.
jokes.setup: Lucille hears about building a wall on the border
jokes.punchline: Takes credit for the idea and admires the payment plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant topical satire connecting the show's plot to real political events. Lucille's narcissism shines.

jokes.text: Hola. Window cleaner! I shall be neither seen nor heard.
jokes.setup: Michael sneaking into his old house
jokes.punchline: Terrible Spanish accent and obvious disguise attempt
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's desperation and poor planning skills.

jokes.text: I blue myself. It's vodka. It goes bad once it's opened. I heard the jury's still out on science.
jokes.setup: Michael finds evidence of his family in the house
jokes.punchline: Series of classic Tobias quotes and malapropisms
jokes.explanation: Greatest hits compilation of Tobias quotes. High nostalgia value for fans.

jokes.text: Guys, it's 9:00. Gives us 30 minutes till they shut down that last machine at Pinkberry.
jokes.setup: Americans about to experience Mexican nightlife
jokes.punchline: Their idea of nightlife is frozen yogurt
jokes.explanation: Good satire of American students abroad prioritizing familiar chains over local culture.

tmdb_overview: For varying reasons, most of the Bluths (even Steve Holt) feel compelled to run off to Mexico. Michael tries to convince Buster to go to the police.
insight: Absurdist plotting carries a mid-tier episode through 59 jokes in steady, character-driven rhythms.
